text:
There was once a little girl named Olive who loved to draw. Her favorite picture to draw was a cozy nest with birds sleeping on fluffed-up feathers.

One day, Olive had a big art exhibit opening. Despite being terrified it wouldn't sell much, she put her nest drawing on the wall near her exhibition. The birds wore tiny little crowns.

As the evening approached, curious birds started coming to visit Olive's exhibit. They fluttered around her nest drawing, their colorful feathers swirling. Olive watched them with big, round eyes.

"Are you busy?" the first bird asked her.

"I'm drawing... My nest... you?" Olive mumbled.

"We like to draw," said a third bird, fluttering its wings. "We could show you our wings!"

Olive giggled. "What wings?"

"They're beautiful! Like peacocks!" said the bird next to the first.

After that, more birds joined the conversation. A pair of owls perched on a branch
